data.output:
# Story 6: Artifact Listing — Search, Sort, Filter (FINAL)

## Diagnosis (current state)

**Backend** (`cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_artifacts.go`, `schema.graphql:1950-2230`):
- `artifacts(projectID, first/after/last/before, mediaType, search)` already supports:
  - Server-side `mediaType` — **exact equality** (resolver line 56), so the UI's `image/*` chip currently matches nothing. Existing bug.
  - Server-side `search` over **title + path** (case-insensitive substring).
  - Default sort: by `Path` ascending, hard-coded.
- Artifact node fields available for sort/filter: `id`, `path`, `title`, `mediaType`, `staleness`, `updatedAt`, `ddxFrontmatter` (JSON), `description`, `generatedBy`.
- **Adjacent infra discovered**: `Query.search` is in the schema with `SearchResult.snippet`, but its resolver still panics (`resolver.go:136`). Command palette has reusable scoring/token matching (`resolver_feat008.go:415,1042`). Document detail has client-side overlay search (`documents/[...path]/+page.svelte:17`). Story 6 should reuse the `SearchResult`/snippet contract rather than invent a new one.
- Cursor encodes only artifact ID; sort changes need client to drop `after` and resolver needs deterministic tie-breaker.

**Frontend** (`routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/artifacts/+page.{ts,svelte}`):
- Filter chips for `mediaType` (URL-persisted, server-side, triggers reload).
- Search box: client-side only over already-loaded edges; URL-persisted via `replaceState`; **does NOT pass `search` to GraphQL** — so search misses anything past the loaded cursor page.
- No sort UI. No staleness filter. No phase/folder/ID-prefix filter. No snippet rendering.
- Reusable URL/load-more patterns exist in beads page (`beads/+layout.svelte:112`).

**FEAT-008** already requires (US-081b at line 390, US-084 at line 539): category chips + URL state (mediaType chips done), full-text across textual content + sidecar metadata + frontmatter (NOT done), snippet highlighting (NOT done).

## Spec changes

**FEAT-008-web-ui.md** — extend US-081b acceptance with sort dropdown, staleness chip, multi-facet composition, URL-state contract; promote US-084 with concrete server-side scope (title+path → +description+frontmatter → +body) and snippet contract via existing `SearchResult.snippet`. Avoid importing the beads "client-side, no round-trip" search language (US-082b at line 440); artifact list is cursor-paginated and must be server-side.

**TD-NNN — artifact full-text search & facet contract** (new): scope decision for content indexing, size caps, text/binary skip rules, deterministic ordering with tie-breaker, and the **facet contract Story 5 must conform to** (URL keys: `q`, `mediaType`, `staleness`, `phase`, `prefix`, `sort`; multi vs single select per facet; GraphQL arg names).

## Implementation surface

### Phase 1 — wire existing capability + sort + staleness (cheap)
- Frontend `q` → GraphQL `search` arg (kill client-side fallback so search covers full set, not just loaded page).
- Fix `mediaType` wildcard semantics: either translate `image/*` to a prefix match in resolver, or remove wildcard chip.
- Add GraphQL `sort: ArtifactSort` enum: `PATH_ASC|PATH_DESC|TITLE_ASC|TITLE_DESC|UPDATED_DESC|UPDATED_ASC|STALENESS`. Every sort uses `(sortKey, id)` tie-breaker.
- Add `staleness: String` arg.
- Frontend: sort dropdown, staleness chip row, URL state for all params, single source of truth in `+page.ts`. Client clears `after` whenever any of `sort/q/mediaType/staleness` changes.

### Phase 2 — metadata search (cheap-medium)
- Extend server `search` to also scan `description` and `ddxFrontmatter` JSON (frontmatter summary already exposed for docs at resolver_artifacts.go:207). No new index.

### Phase 3 — full-text body + snippets (TD-gated)
- Reuse existing `SearchResult.snippet` shape. Add `ArtifactEdge.snippet: String`.
- On-the-fly grep over textual artifacts at query time, size cap (e.g. 256 KB/file), skip binary, deterministic ordering. Document upgrade path to bleve/sqlite-FTS when artifact count exceeds threshold.
- Frontend: snippet rendering with highlighted match terms.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Given I type a query, the search hits the **entire** project (not just loaded page); URL persists `q=`.
- Given I select a sort option, the list reorders, `sort=` persists, pagination resets cleanly with deterministic tie-breaker.
- Given I apply staleness=stale, only stale artifacts show; chip state persists.
- Given I combine `mediaType + staleness + sort + q`, all four compose; pasting the URL reproduces the same view.
- Given I clear all filters, URL resets to `/artifacts`.
- Given the Images chip is selected, `image/png`/`image/svg+xml` artifacts both appear (wildcard fix verified).
- Given Story 5 lands axes (`phase`, `prefix`), they slot into the published facet contract without re-architecture.
- (Phase 3) Given a query matches body text, I see the artifact with a highlighted snippet.

## Tests

**Unit (Go):** `resolver_artifacts_test.go` — sort orderings + tie-breaker stability, staleness filter, search composition (title/path/description/frontmatter), wildcard mediaType match, pagination correctness across filtered+sorted set.

**Unit (Vitest):** URL-state round-trip (parse/serialize) for all params; sort dropdown emits expected query; load-more preserves all query state.

**E2E (Playwright):** `artifacts.spec.ts` — server-side search matches across pages; sort change resets pagination; staleness chip filters; URL copy/reload restores state; (Phase 3) snippet highlight visible.

**Perf (Go bench):** B4a benchmark — 500-artifact fixture with size caps, p95 budget for content search; verify worst-case stays bounded.

## Risks

- **Cursor + filter/sort interaction:** server-side filters/sort invalidate cursors. Mitigation: client drops `after` on any param change; resolver enforces `(sortKey, id)` tie-breaker.
- **Wildcard chip currently broken** — must be fixed in B1, not deferred.
- **Story 5 coupling:** must publish facet contract NOW (in TD-NNN) so Story 5 grouping names don't drift from Story 6 filter URL keys.
- **Search semantics:** simple substring v1; boolean/regex out of scope; document the choice.
- **B4 size:** split into B4a/B4b/B4c (TD+server, GraphQL contract+resolver, frontend+e2e).

## Bead Breakdown

| # | Title | Deps | Tier |
|---|-------|------|------|
| B1 | Fix mediaType wildcard match + wire frontend `q` to server `search` arg + add `sort` enum and `staleness` arg with tie-breaker | none | cheap (deterministic) |
| B2 | Frontend sort dropdown + staleness chips + URL state contract for all filter params; client resets `after` on any param change | B1 | cheap |
| B3 | Publish facet contract (TD-NNN section) + add `phase` / `prefix` filter args + chips wired to Story 5 axes | Story 5 final, B2 | medium |
| B4a | TD-NNN: artifact search semantics (title→path→description→frontmatter→body), size caps, binary skip, deterministic ordering, perf benchmark fixture | B1 | strong (design + measurement) |
| B4b | GraphQL: extend `search` to metadata fields + add `ArtifactEdge.snippet` (reuse `SearchResult.snippet` shape) + resolver tests | B4a | medium |
| B4c | Frontend snippet rendering + highlight + e2e for full-text path and back-navigation | B4b | cheap-medium |

Suggested execution: B1 → B2 in fast lane; B4a kicks off in parallel as a TD; B3 waits on Story 5 final; B4b → B4c gated by B4a.

## Coordination with Story 5

Story 5 defines categorization axes (folder, frontmatter, HELIX phase). Story 6 turns axes into filter facets.

**Action item from codex review:** Story 6 publishes a facet contract NOW (in TD-NNN, before B3) so Story 5 cannot drift:
- URL keys reserved by Story 6: `q`, `mediaType`, `staleness`, `phase`, `prefix`, `sort`.
- Multi vs single select: `mediaType` single (today), `staleness` single, `phase` single, `prefix` multi.
- Source fields: `phase` derived from path prefix `docs/helix/NN-*/`; `prefix` derived from `id` prefix segment (`ADR|SD|FEAT|US|RSCH|...`).
- Filter narrows; Story 5 grouping organizes the remaining set. Both compose without collision.

## Flagged ambiguities (unresolved)

1. **Search scope final v1:** title+path only (today), or title+path+description+frontmatter? Recommendation: ship title+path (B1) then immediately metadata (B4b) — only body text is gated by TD.
2. **Server vs client execution:** decided **server-side** (cursor pagination forces it). Documented.
3. **URL state persistence:** decided **deep-linkable URL** (no session-only state). Documented.
4. **Boolean/regex search:** out of scope v1. Substring only.
5. **Wildcard mediaType:** fix-in-place (translate `image/*` to prefix in resolver) or drop chip — needs decision in B1.
6. **Phase axis source of truth:** path-prefix derivation vs explicit frontmatter field — needs Story 5 alignment.

1	import type { PageLoad } from './$types'
2	import { createClient } from '$lib/gql/client'
3	import { gql } from 'graphql-request'
4	import { readState } from '$lib/urlState'
5	import type { GroupBy } from './grouping'
6	
7	export const ARTIFACTS_QUERY = gql`
8		query Artifacts($projectID: ID!, $first: Int, $after: String, $mediaType: String, $search: String, $sort: ArtifactSort, $staleness: String) {
9			artifacts(projectID: $projectID, first: $first, after: $after, mediaType: $mediaType, search: $search, sort: $sort, staleness: $staleness) {
10				edges {
11					node {
12						id
13						path
14						title
15						mediaType
16						staleness
17					}
18					cursor
19				}
20				pageInfo {
21					hasNextPage
22					endCursor
23				}
24				totalCount
25			}
26		}
27	`
28	
29	export interface ArtifactNode {
30		id: string
31		path: string
32		title: string
33		mediaType: string
34		staleness: string
35	}
36	
37	export interface ArtifactEdge {
38		node: ArtifactNode
39		cursor: string
40	}
41	
42	export interface PageInfo {
43		hasNextPage: boolean
44		endCursor: string | null
45	}
46	
47	export interface ArtifactConnection {
48		edges: ArtifactEdge[]
49		pageInfo: PageInfo
50		totalCount: number
51	}
52	
53	interface ArtifactsResult {
54		artifacts: ArtifactConnection
55	}
56	
57	export const PAGE_SIZE = 50
58	
59	export const SORT_OPTIONS = [
60		{ value: 'PATH', label: 'Path' },
61		{ value: 'TITLE', label: 'Title' },
62		{ value: 'MODIFIED', label: 'Last modified' },
63		{ value: 'DEPS_COUNT', label: 'Dependency count' },
64		{ value: 'ID', label: 'ID' }
65	] as const
66	
67	export type ArtifactSort = (typeof SORT_OPTIONS)[number]['value']
68	export const DEFAULT_SORT: ArtifactSort = 'PATH'
69	
70	const SORT_VALUES = new Set(SORT_OPTIONS.map((o) => o.value))
71	
72	export function parseSort(raw: string | null): ArtifactSort {
73		return raw && SORT_VALUES.has(raw as ArtifactSort) ? (raw as ArtifactSort) : DEFAULT_SORT
74	}
75	
76	export const STALENESS_OPTIONS = ['fresh', 'stale', 'missing'] as const
77	export type Staleness = (typeof STALENESS_OPTIONS)[number]
78	const STALENESS_SET = new Set<string>(STALENESS_OPTIONS)
79	
80	export function parseStaleness(raw: string | null): Staleness | null {
81		return raw && STALENESS_SET.has(raw) ? (raw as Staleness) : null
82	}
83	
84	export const load: PageLoad = async ({ params, url, fetch }) => {
85		const state = readState(url.searchParams)
86		const mediaType = state.mediaType
87		const q = state.q
88		const groupBy: GroupBy = state.groupBy
89		const sort = parseSort(state.sort)
90		const staleness = parseStaleness(state.staleness)
91	
92		const client = createClient(fetch as unknown as typeof globalThis.fetch)
93		const data = await client.request<ArtifactsResult>(ARTIFACTS_QUERY, {
94			projectID: params.projectId,
95			first: PAGE_SIZE,
96			mediaType: mediaType ?? undefined,
97			search: q ? q : undefined,
98			sort,
99			staleness: staleness ?? undefined
100		})
101		return {
102			nodeId: params.nodeId,
103			projectId: params.projectId,
104			artifacts: data.artifacts,
105			mediaType,
106			q,
107			groupBy,
108			sort,
109			staleness
110		}
111	}
112

cli/internal/server/frontend/src/lib/urlState.ts
cli/internal/server/frontend/src/lib/urlState.test.ts

1	import { describe, it, expect } from 'vitest'
2	import { readState, writeState, backHref, DEFAULT_GROUP_BY } from './urlState'
3	
4	describe('urlState.readState', () => {
5		it('returns defaults for an empty URL', () => {
6			const s = readState(new URLSearchParams(''))
7			expect(s.q).toBe('')
8			expect(s.mediaType).toBeNull()
9			expect(s.groupBy).toBe(DEFAULT_GROUP_BY)
10			expect(s.sort).toBeNull()
11			expect(s.staleness).toBeNull()
12			expect(s.filters).toEqual({})
13		})
14	
15		it('parses known keys and filter.* entries', () => {
16			const s = readState(
17				new URLSearchParams(
18					'q=foo&mediaType=text/markdown&groupBy=prefix&sort=title&staleness=stale&filter.tag=v1'
19				)
20			)
21			expect(s.q).toBe('foo')
22			expect(s.mediaType).toBe('text/markdown')
23			expect(s.groupBy).toBe('prefix')
24			expect(s.sort).toBe('title')
25			expect(s.staleness).toBe('stale')
26			expect(s.filters).toEqual({ tag: 'v1' })
27		})
28	
29		it('falls back to default groupBy on unknown values', () => {
30			const s = readState(new URLSearchParams('groupBy=nonsense'))
31			expect(s.groupBy).toBe(DEFAULT_GROUP_BY)
32		})
33	})
34	
35	describe('urlState.writeState', () => {
36		it('preserves unrelated keys, including back', () => {
37			const next = writeState(new URLSearchParams('back=/x?y=1&other=keep'), { q: 'hi' })
38			expect(next.get('back')).toBe('/x?y=1')
39			expect(next.get('other')).toBe('keep')
40			expect(next.get('q')).toBe('hi')
41		})
42	
43		it('drops groupBy when it equals the default', () => {
44			const next = writeState(new URLSearchParams('groupBy=prefix'), { groupBy: 'folder' })
45			expect(next.has('groupBy')).toBe(false)
46		})
47	
48		it('deletes keys when given empty/null values', () => {
49			const next = writeState(new URLSearchParams('q=old&mediaType=x'), {
50				q: '',
51				mediaType: null
52			})
53			expect(next.has('q')).toBe(false)
54			expect(next.has('mediaType')).toBe(false)
55		})
56	
57		it('round-trips sort and staleness, and clears them on null', () => {
58			const set = writeState(new URLSearchParams(''), { sort: 'TITLE', staleness: 'stale' })
59			expect(set.get('sort')).toBe('TITLE')
60			expect(set.get('staleness')).toBe('stale')
61			expect(readState(set).sort).toBe('TITLE')
62			expect(readState(set).staleness).toBe('stale')
63	
64			const cleared = writeState(set, { sort: null, staleness: null })
65			expect(cleared.has('sort')).toBe(false)
66			expect(cleared.has('staleness')).toBe(false)
67		})
68	
69		it('replaces filter.* entries atomically', () => {
70			const next = writeState(new URLSearchParams('filter.a=1&filter.b=2&q=keep'), {
71				filters: { c: '3' }
72			})
73			expect(next.has('filter.a')).toBe(false)
74			expect(next.has('filter.b')).toBe(false)
75			expect(next.get('filter.c')).toBe('3')
76			expect(next.get('q')).toBe('keep')
77		})
78	})
79	
80	describe('urlState.backHref', () => {
81		it('omits the trailing ? when there are no params', () => {
82			expect(backHref('/x', new URLSearchParams(''))).toBe('/x')
83		})
84		it('joins path and params', () => {
85			expect(backHref('/x', new URLSearchParams('q=1'))).toBe('/x?q=1')
86		})
87	})

d0a5dec0 feat(web): artifacts sort dropdown + staleness chips, cursor reset on param change [ddx-5af9ae07]

commit d0a5dec0ecf9a377e19ff1009c969710de9bd535
Author: Test User <test@example.com>
Date:   Sat May 2 09:10:29 2026 -0400

    feat(web): artifacts sort dropdown + staleness chips, cursor reset on param change [ddx-5af9ae07]
    
    Adds a sort-by dropdown and staleness filter chips to the artifacts list.
    Both round-trip through the shared urlState helper (extended with a known
    `staleness` key). All param mutations route through goto(), so SvelteKit
    re-runs load() and the cursor naturally resets via the data-bound $effect.
    Search now debounces and uses goto({ replaceState: true }) instead of a
    bare history.replaceState, so search edits also refetch from the server
    and reset the cursor. e2e covers sort/filter/search composition.

- Sort dropdown + staleness chips: `+page.svelte` lines 220–245, 261–274
- URL contract (q/mediaType/staleness/sort): `urlState.ts` lines 7–88
- Cursor reset via goto() reload: `+page.svelte` `navigateWith()` line 67
- Bare URL on clear: `writeState` deletes empty values
- Vitest round-trip: `urlState.test.ts` lines 57–67

The only test failure (`D3Graph.contrast.spec.ts` stroke-opacity) is pre-existing and unrelated to artifact filter UI.

input.content:
Bead ddx-baa1dbf2 — Story 6 B2 — no changes required.

The bead's full scope was already implemented and merged before this attempt, by
prior bead ddx-5af9ae07 in commit d0a5dec0 ("feat(web): artifacts sort dropdown
+ staleness chips, cursor reset on param change"). The base commit for this
attempt (bb783e63) is downstream of that merge, so the worktree already
satisfies every acceptance criterion:

AC mapping (file paths relative to repo root):

1. "Sort dropdown reorders list; URL persists sort=."
   - cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/artifacts/+page.svelte
     lines 261–274 (sort <select> bound to data.sort, onchange → selectSort →
     navigateWith({ sort })).
   - cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/artifacts/+page.ts
     lines 59–74 (SORT_OPTIONS, ArtifactSort, parseSort, DEFAULT_SORT) and the
     load() function passes sort to the GraphQL request.
   - cli/internal/server/frontend/src/lib/urlState.ts lines 7, 36, 61–64
     (sort key parse/serialize; cleared when null/empty).

2. "Staleness chip filters; URL persists staleness=."
   - +page.svelte lines 220–245 (staleness chips with data-testid hooks,
     selectStaleness toggle, clear button).
   - +page.ts lines 76–82 (STALENESS_OPTIONS / parseStaleness).
   - urlState.ts lines 7, 37, 65–68 (staleness key parse/serialize).

3. "q/mediaType/staleness/sort compose; pasting a URL with all four reproduces
   the same view."
   - load() in +page.ts (lines 84–111) is the single source of truth: it reads
     all four params from the URL via readState and forwards them as GraphQL
     variables, so a deep link reproduces the same query.
   - navigateWith() in +page.svelte (lines 67–78) merges patches into the
     current URLSearchParams and triggers goto(), so combinations compose.

4. "Clearing all filters returns URL to /artifacts."
   - writeState in urlState.ts lines 49–68 deletes a key whenever the patched
     value is empty/null. navigateWith() rebuilds the path with no query
     string when params.toString() is empty (+page.svelte lines 72–77).

5. "Reset 'after' cursor whenever any of q/sort/mediaType/staleness changes."
   - All param mutations route through navigateWith() → goto(), which re-runs
     load() without any 'after' arg; the $effect on `data` (lines 30–33 of
     +page.svelte) replaces allEdges and pageInfo, dropping the cursor. The
     debounced search path also calls fetchSeq.invalidate() (lines 80–89) so
     any in-flight loadMore is discarded.

6. "Vitest URL-state round-trip test for parse/serialize of all params."
   - cli/internal/server/frontend/src/lib/urlState.test.ts lines 16–27 (parses
     q, mediaType, groupBy, sort, staleness, filter.* together) and lines
     57–67 (round-trips sort + staleness, clears them on null).
   - vitest run from this worktree: urlState.test.ts passes (the only failing
     unit test is D3Graph.contrast.spec.ts, which is unrelated — it asserts
     no stroke-opacity in D3Graph.svelte but the current source contains
     stroke-opacity from prior bead ddx-7e7b12f0; out of scope for this bead).

7. "Playwright e2e covers: server-side q matches across pages, sort change
   resets pagination, staleness chip filters, URL copy/reload restores state."
   - cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/artifacts.spec.ts test "artifacts: sort
     + staleness chips + search compose into URL state and refetch"
     (lines 222–355) covers sort change refetch with after=null, staleness
     chip filter with after=null, q composition with sort+staleness, and chip
     toggle-off. The earlier US-081b test (lines 156–206) covers server-side
     q in conjunction with navigation.

8. "URL contract: q (string), mediaType (single), staleness (single), sort
   (single ArtifactSort value)."
   - urlState.ts encodes exactly this contract; only a single value is read
     per key (params.get returns the first), and writeState writes one value
     per key.

No code changes are needed in this attempt. Per the execute-bead instructions,
"a well-justified no_changes is better than a bad commit." Re-implementing the
already-merged work would only churn the diff and risk regressions.

Pre-existing unit test failure (out of scope for this bead):
- src/lib/components/D3Graph.contrast.spec.ts asserts the rendered
  D3Graph.svelte source contains no `stroke-opacity`, but the current source
  (line 302) sets stroke-opacity=0.9. That test was added in commit 3b0fefc1
  for bead ddx-7e7b12f0 and is a pre-existing failure unrelated to artifact
  filter UI.
